meta.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept CPBS items for SpeedyCAT (chunk 8 of 10). NOT real MCAT/AAMC content and NOT from any answer key. Every stem and choice is plain text (no HTML/SVG/LaTeX). Answer keys were computed programmatically and each item passed a 4-criteria eval (four distinct choices; clean plain-text formatting; MCAT-appropriate difficulty; exactly one correct choice).

questions.stem: Using PV = nRT (R = 0.0821 L·atm/(mol·K)), what is the pressure of 1 mol of gas in 5 L at 400 K?

questions.stem: Resonance structures are used to describe a molecule when

choices.label: A
choices.text: the molecule contains only single bonds.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the molecule can only be drawn one way.

choices.label: C
choices.text: bonding electrons are delocalized over more than one valid Lewis structure.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the octet rule is always violated.

questions.explanation: Resonance represents delocalized bonding as an average of contributing Lewis structures.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Percent yield is calculated as
questions.explanation: Percent yield compares measured product to the maximum predicted by stoichiometry.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas at 1 atm occupies 4 L. At constant temperature, what volume does it occupy at 2.5 atm?
questions.explanation: Boyle's law: P1 V1 = P2 V2, so V2 = (1)(4)/2.5 = 1.6 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much heat is required to boil (vaporize) 5 g of water? (latent heat = 2260 J/g)
questions.explanation: q = m L = (5)(2260) = 11300 J for the phase change (no temperature change).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the gauge pressure at a depth of 2 m in a fluid of density 1025 kg/m^3? (g = 9.8 m/s^2)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Electronegativity generally increases

choices.label: B
choices.text: toward the upper right of the periodic table (excluding noble gases).

choices.label: D
choices.text: toward the lower left of the periodic table.

questions.explanation: Fluorine (upper right) is most electronegative; electronegativity rises up and to the right.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A wave of frequency 1000 Hz travels at 1500 m/s. What is its wavelength?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to VSEPR theory, what is the molecular geometry of HCN?
questions.explanation: HCN adopts a linear geometry based on its bonding and lone-pair electron domains.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the approximate molar mass of NaOH? (Use H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, Na=23, Cl=35.5, S=32, K=39, Ca=40, Mg=24, Fe=56, Ag=108)
questions.explanation: Summing the atomic masses in NaOH gives a molar mass of about 40 g/mol.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the conjugate acid of the base NH3?
questions.explanation: Adding a proton to NH3 gives the ammonium ion NH4+, its conjugate acid.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A reaction proceeds in two steps with ΔH1 = 150 kJ and ΔH2 = 250 kJ. If the overall reaction is the sum of these steps, what is the overall ΔH?
questions.explanation: By Hess's law, ΔH_overall = ΔH1 + ΔH2 = 150 + 250 = 400 kJ.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 3 m/s through a pipe of cross-section 8 cm^2. What is its speed where the cross-section narrows to 4 cm^2?
questions.explanation: Continuity: A1 v1 = A2 v2, so v2 = (8)(3)/4 = 6 m/s.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many protons are in an atom of iron-56?
questions.explanation: The number of protons equals the atomic number; for iron-56 that is 26.

questions.stem: What is the hybridization of the central atom in PCl3?
questions.explanation: The electron-domain geometry of PCl3 corresponds to sp3 hybridization of the central atom.

questions.stem: What is the mass of 0.1 mol of NaOH? (molar mass 40 g/mol)
questions.explanation: mass = moles x molar mass = 0.1 x 40 = 4 g.

choices.label: D
choices.text: always lowers the system's potential energy.

questions.explanation: Bond breaking absorbs energy; bond forming releases energy.

questions.stem: An object floats in a fluid when its average density is

choices.label: A
choices.text: less than the density of the fluid.

choices.label: C
choices.text: greater than the density of the fluid.

questions.explanation: If the object is less dense than the fluid, buoyancy exceeds weight and it floats.

questions.stem: What is the mass number of iron-56?
questions.explanation: The mass number counts protons plus neutrons; for iron-56 it is 56.

questions.explanation: silicon (Si) is in a main group whose number of valence electrons is 4.

questions.stem: How many molecules are in 0.2 mol of a substance? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)
questions.explanation: molecules = moles x N_A = 0.2 x 6.022 x 10^23 = 1.2 x 10^23.

questions.stem: A system absorbs 500 J of heat and does 100 J of work on its surroundings. What is the change in its internal energy?
questions.explanation: First law: ΔU = Q - W = 500 - 100 = 400 J.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many electrons are in the ion F-? (atomic number 9)
questions.explanation: Adjust the neutral atom's 9 electrons for the charge: F- has 10 electrons.

questions.stem: Which pair correctly classifies these as vector and scalar?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both velocity and speed are vectors.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Velocity is a vector; speed is a scalar.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both velocity and speed are scalars.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Speed is a vector; velocity is a scalar.

questions.explanation: Velocity has direction (vector); speed is its magnitude only (scalar).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: The bond in I2 has an electronegativity difference of about 0. It is best classified as
questions.explanation: An electronegativity difference near 0 indicates a nonpolar covalent bond (roughly: 0 nonpolar covalent, up to about 1.7 polar covalent, larger values ionic).

questions.stem: What is the mass percent of Cl in NaCl?
questions.explanation: mass percent = (mass of element / molar mass) x 100 = (35.5/58.5) x 100 = 60.7%.

questions.explanation: SO2 is a polar molecule because its dipoles do not cancel, giving a net dipole moment.

questions.stem: In the visible spectrum, which color of light has the highest frequency?
questions.explanation: Violet has the shortest wavelength and highest frequency; red has the lowest.

questions.stem: What is the strongest type of intermolecular force present in pure CH3F?
questions.explanation: The strongest intermolecular force in CH3F is dipole-dipole forces.

questions.stem: Convert 1.5 kg to grams.

questions.stem: A gas at 1.5 atm occupies 4 L. At constant temperature, what volume does it occupy at 1 atm?
questions.explanation: Boyle's law: P1 V1 = P2 V2, so V2 = (1.5)(4)/1 = 6 L.

questions.stem: A reaction proceeds in two steps with ΔH1 = -200 kJ and ΔH2 = -50 kJ. If the overall reaction is the sum of these steps, what is the overall ΔH?
questions.explanation: By Hess's law, ΔH_overall = ΔH1 + ΔH2 = -200 + -50 = -250 kJ.

questions.stem: The octet rule states that many atoms tend to gain, lose, or share electrons to achieve

choices.label: B
choices.text: an odd number of valence electrons.

choices.label: C
choices.text: two valence electrons in all cases.

questions.explanation: Main-group atoms are most stable with a filled octet resembling the nearest noble gas.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many significant figures are in the measurement 0.00405 kg?
questions.explanation: Leading zeros are not significant; 4, 0, and 5 are the three significant figures.

questions.stem: A neutralization reaction between a strong acid and a strong base produces

choices.label: B

questions.explanation: Acid + base neutralization yields a salt (from the ions) plus water.

questions.stem: The activation energy of a reaction is

choices.label: A
